docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT
MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT BOURQUE, JAMES HEARING SET FOR MAY-22-2015 AT 03:30 PM IN DEPT 506
(SC-105 ANSWER) TO REQUEST FOR COURT ORDER AND ANSWER FILED BY PLAINTIFF KRIPPENDORF, DAVID

THE ORDER OF EXAMINATION IS DISCHARGED AS TO DEFENDANT, JAMES BOURQUE. SATISFACTION OF JUDGMENT FILED OCTOBER 18, 2016. NO APPEARANCES. JUDGE: DONALD S. MITCHELL. CLERK: J. BERTELSEN. NOT REPORTED. (506)

MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT FILED BY JAMES BOURQUE ON MAY-22-2015 - DENIED. "FAILURE TO APPEAR BY DEFENDANT JAMES BOURQUE", BY PRO TEM JUDGE MICHAEL MAU. APPEARANCE BY PLAINTIFF, ONLY. PLAINTIFFS EXHIBITS NOT RETAINED. DEFENDANTS EXHIBITS NOT RETAINED. JUDGE PRO TEM: MICHAEL L. MAU. CLERK: P. NATT. NOT REPORTED. (506)

SMALL CLAIMS OEX CALENDAR OF SEP-15-2016 IS CONTINUED PURSUANT TO STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES TO NOV-03-2016 AT 11:00 AM IN DEPT. 506 FOR PROGRESS REPORT. APPEARANCES BY PLAINTIFF DAVID KRIPPENDORF & DEFENDANT JAMES BOURQUE. PROOF OF SERVICE ON FILE. PARTIES REACHED A SETTLEMENT WHEREIN DEFENDANT SHALL PAY THE FULL JUDGMENT ON OR BEFORE OCTOBER 15, 2016. THE COURT SHALL DISMISS THIS MATTER WITH PREJUDICE IF NEITHER PARTY APPEARS AT THE NEXT CALLING OF THE CASE ON 11/3/16. DEFENDANT JAMES BOURQUE'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S 5/22/15 ORDER DENYING THE MOTION TO VACATE THE JUDGMENT IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID LAVINE. CLERK: K. DOUGHERTY. NOT REPORTED. (D506).
PROOF OF SERVICE OF OEX AND STATEMENT OF ASSETS FILED BY PLAINTIFF KRIPPENDORF, DAVID SERVED MAY-20-2016, SUBSTITUTE SERVICE ON NATURAL PERSON AS TO DEFENDANT BOURQUE, JAMES

SMALL CLAIMS OEX CALENDAR OF JUL-14-2016 IS CONTINUED PURSUANT TO STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES TO SEP-15-2016 AT 11:00 AM IN DEPT. 506 FOR FURTHER ORDER OF EXAMINATION. APPEARANCES BY PLAINTIFF DAVID KRIPPENDORF & DEFENDANT JAMES BOURQUE. PROOF OF SERVICE OF OEX IN THE FILE. DEFENDANT JAMES BOURQUE WAIVES NOTICE OF THE NEXT HEARING DATE. JUDGE: DONALD S. MITCHELL. CLERK: K. DOUGHERTY. NOT REPORTED. (D506).
(SC-105) REQUEST FOR COURT ORDER AND ANSWER FILED BY DEFENDANT BOURQUE, JAMES
ORDER (SC-105A) ORDER ON REQUEST FOR COURT ORDER: THE REQUEST FOR COURT ORDER TO ALLOW MOTION TO VACATE BE REHEARD/RECALANDERED FILED ON 9-13-16 IS GRANTED.
AUTO ACCIDENT CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF FOR $4,388.11 FILED BY KRIPPENDORF, DAVID AS TO DEFENDANT BOURQUE, JAMES ORDER SETTING TRIAL ISSUED FOR FEB-23-2015 AT 01:30 PM IN DEPT 506

SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF FEB-23-2015 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF FEB-23-2015 BY JUDGE PRO TEM DAVID LAVINE. APPEARANCE BY PLAINTIFF, ONLY. PLAINTIFFS EXHIBITS NOT RETAINED. DEFENDANTS EXHIBITS NOT RETAINED. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID LAVINE. CLERK: P. NATT. NOT REPORTED. (506)
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF FEB-23-2015 - JUDGMENT.
STIPULATION FOR HEARING BEFORE PRO TEM JUDGE MICHAEL MAU. FILED BY KRIPPENDORF, DAVID
COPYING FEES (10 PAGE) PAID BY PLAINTIFF KRIPPENDORF, DAVID
STIPULATION FOR HEARING BEFORE PRO TEM JUDGE DAVID LAVINE. FILED BY KRIPPENDORF, DAVID
COPYING FEES (1 PAGE, 1 CERTIFIED COPY) (NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT) PAID BY PLAINTIFF KRIPPENDORF, DAVID
COPYING FEES (1 CERTIFIED COPY) PAID BY PLAINTIFF KRIPPENDORF, DAVID
PROOF OF SERVICE FILED ON CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF - FEE FOR SERVICE $ 52.50 - SERVED JAN-23-2015, SUBSTITUTE SERVICE ON NATURAL PERSON AS TO DEFENDANT BOURQUE, JAMES
DECLARATION OF DUE DILIGENCE IN ATTEMPTING SERVICE - FEE FOR SERVICE $ 52.50 FILED BY PLAINTIFF KRIPPENDORF, DAVID AS TO DEFENDANT BOURQUE, JAMES

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF KRIPPENDORF, DAVID RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT BOURQUE, JAMES $4,257.79 PRINCIPAL, $102.50 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $4,360.29, DEFENDANT DID NOT APPEAR. PLAINTIFF SUBSTANTIATED MOST OF HIS DAMAGES CLAIM. STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS NO EXHIBITS FILED
D.M.V. CERTIFICATE OF FACTS RE UNSATISFIED JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF KRIPPENDORF, DAVID AS TO DEFENDANT BOURQUE, JAMES
ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED AS TO DEFENDANT BOURQUE, JAMES HEARING SET FOR JUL-14-2016 AT 11:00 AM IN DEPT 506
ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF SATISFACTION OF JUDGMENT, FULLY SATISFIED, AS TO DEFENDANT BOURQUE, JAMES
